items.q: What is the scope of a local variable?
items.correct: Accessible only within the function/block where it is declared.
items.random_wrong: Stored on a local computer rather than a server.
items.why: Students confuse 'local' with 'on this computer'. In programming, scope refers to the CODE REGION where the variable name resolves - local variables vanish when the function returns.

items.q: Why must a recursive function have a base case?
items.correct: Without it, the function calls itself indefinitely, causing a stack overflow.
items.random_wrong: To make the function run faster.
items.why: Students treat the base case as optional ceremony. It's the TERMINATION CONDITION that stops recursion - each call adds a stack frame, and without termination the stack will overflow.

items.q: What is the denary value of binary 10110?
items.why: Students forget to apply place values. Each position is a power of 2 from right (1, 2, 4, 8, 16...). Reading binary as denary is the most common error.

items.q: Which higher-order function transforms every element?
items.why: It's Map. “Filter” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.correct: A base case and a recursive case
items.why: Correct answer: A base case and a recursive case. “Two base cases” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many essential parts does a recursive function need (base + recursive)?
items.why: Exact answer: 2. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
